items.mistake: Scaling the full engineered matrix in a notebook, then passing arrays to cross_val_score without a Pipeline
items.fix: Wrap imputer, scaler, and model in one Pipeline; call cross_val_score on the Pipeline so each fold refits scaler statistics on inner-train engineered rows only.
items.why: Pre-scaling before CV lets validation folds influence μ and σ, leaking holdout distribution into preprocessing.
items.example: StandardScaler().fit_transform(X_engineered) on all rows, then KNeighborsClassifier in CV—validation neighbors see z-scores computed with their own statistics.

items.mistake: Applying StandardScaler to one-hot encoded categoricals in the same branch as engineered numerics
items.fix: Keep categoricals in a separate ColumnTransformer branch with encoder only; scale continuous engineered columns in the numeric branch.
items.why: Scaling binary indicator columns distorts their 0/1 semantics and adds noise without helping distance or penalties.
items.example: A 47-column one-hot `region` block after StandardScaler no longer has unit variance per category and breaks interpretability.

items.mistake: Always adding StandardScaler after feature engineering regardless of algorithm
items.fix: Match the scale branch to algorithm sensitivity: include for k-NN, Ridge, SVM, NNs; omit for pure tree/boosting on engineered numerics unless a downstream step needs it.
items.why: Trees are scale-invariant on monotone transforms; unnecessary scalers add complexity without changing splits.
items.example: HistGradientBoostingClassifier inside Pipeline([('scale', StandardScaler()), ('hgb', ...)]) on `txn_ratio` and `count_7d`—extra step, identical predictions.

items.mistake: Serializing the estimator without the fitted scaler after engineering new numeric columns
items.fix: Save the full fitted Pipeline or ColumnTransformer via joblib; version the artifact whenever engineered schema or scaler columns change.
items.why: Production must apply the same train-learned transform to engineered features; a bare model on pre-scaled arrays cannot reproduce training.
items.example: joblib.dump(ridge_model, 'model.joblib') while StandardScaler was fit in a separate cell—serving passes raw `log_revenue` into Ridge.
